facts: PatchProof is a locally runnable browser application at immutable revision ae88d46686b23b6374899c5f0606e17859b93b99.
The documented setup is npm ci followed by npm run dev; npm run verify is the integrated proof command.
Fresh verification passed 34 unit assertions, 12 complete fixture contracts, a production build, and desktop/mobile browser flows.
The product derives five distinct claim states and rejects malformed JSON import without replacing the current draft.
The Working Web App matches the PatchProof opportunity selected by the archived Discovery run.
hypotheses: Developers will value a lightweight manual receipt builder across agents.
A browser-only local workflow will be easier than reviewing raw logs.
Portable Markdown and JSON receipts will fit existing review habits.
decisions: Advance PatchProof—not a rejected alternative—to launch-package eligibility.
Use verified product behavior and preserved repair evidence as the launch proof surface.
Let Developer Project Launch derive three project-specific creative directions; do not inherit a stock aesthetic.
constraints: Do not change confirmed product behavior, five-state semantics, run commands, evidence, or claims during creative Remix.
No deployment, publication, outreach, analytics, customer-data collection, spending, or other external mutation is authorized.
Do not call the product secure, production-ready, market-validated, or proven to have demand.
recordedMismatches: No material mismatch exists between the selected PatchProof opportunity and the built primary flow.
Discovery proposed later developer testing; this local build did not run or claim that external validation.

checks: local run and integrated verification commands are documented
primary browser flow passed fresh verification
opportunity alignment and remaining mismatches are recorded
claims, hypotheses, unknowns, and external boundaries remain distinct

authorization.approvedScope: Reviewed repo-local skill installation and local Developer Project Launch preparation for PatchProof only. No deployment, publication, push, tagging, release creation, DNS, analytics, outreach, data collection, spending, or provider mutation.
